name: Mountain Home AFB
profile: Military-friendly Mountain Home AFB housing snapshot anchored to Mountain Home / Elmore County, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 Idaho VA loan rates.

notes: Property taxes and insurance materially affect true monthly ownership cost near Mountain Home.
Insurance and HOA should be included in all-in housing cost comparisons.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate; low property taxes in Idaho significantly enhance purchasing power for base personnel.

crime_status.metro_shreveport_bossier: Mountain Home is a very safe rural community; property crime is the primary, though infrequent, concern.

school_quality.on_base: Base Primary School (Mountain Home SD #193) serves on-base residents.
school_quality.off_base: Mountain Home School District schools are the standard; many families prefer the newer elementary options.

rental_vacancy.notes: Healthy supply of single-family rentals in the city; inventory is tighter on base.

rental_metrics.notes: Healthy supply of single-family rentals in the city; inventory is tighter on base.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: True military town feel; the community is highly focused on supporting Gunfighter personnel.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Low entry costs and low taxes make this a strong market for first-time VA buyers.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest in the newer subdivisions on the northern edge of the city.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Mountain Home offers consistent value and a very low cost of living for military families.

summary_points: Mountain Home Air Force Base sits in the Mountain Home / Elmore County housing market with base-specific affordability, commute, and neighborhood tradeoffs.
Current market trend is approximately 3.8% year-over-year, which should shape buyer leverage and pricing discipline.
Low entry costs and low taxes make this a strong market for first-time VA buyers.
Neighborhood quality varies across Mountain Home, Boise corridor, Eagle / Meridian alternatives and other nearby corridors — citywide averages should not be the only filter.
Mountain Home offers consistent value and a very low cost of living for military families.
opportunities: Mountain Home military demand can support durable housing relevance for disciplined buyers and landlords.
Local inventory and pricing may offer opportunity for households that stress-test taxes, insurance, and commute together.
Mountain Home and nearby corridors may offer stronger family or value positioning depending on assignment length.
Assignment-length clarity can improve rent-versus-buy decisions near Mountain Home.
risks: Neighborhood quality, schools, commute, and monthly carrying costs vary across the Mountain Home / Elmore County market.
Property taxes, insurance, HOA, and utilities can widen the gap between list price and true monthly payment.
Market averages can hide weak submarkets — property-level review matters.
Stretching beyond a safe housing ratio can reduce PCS flexibility.
buyer_notes: Mountain Home buyers should compare all-in monthly cost, not just principal and interest.
Verify school zones, commute timing, and BAH fit by exact address before signing.
Strongest in the newer subdivisions on the northern edge of the city.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who test the commute during expected report hours.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against current inventory and days on market rather than older peak assumptions.
Move-in-ready presentation and realistic pricing matter when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
investor_angles: Military turnover near Mountain Home can support rental demand, but vacancy and maintenance should be modeled conservatively.
Investors should underwrite taxes, insurance, HOA, and property condition — not just acquisition price.
Practical commute corridors often outperform generic metro averages for military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not choose housing based only on distance to Mountain Home.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market.
Condition and presentation matter when buyers can compare more options.
Military commute convenience can help positioning if described accurately.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Mountain Home.
Model vacancy, repairs, insurance, and turnover conservatively.
Stable spread matters more than headline affordability alone.

base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Mountain Home AFB is a high-desert fighter installation supporting Gunfighter operations with a tight military-town housing market and Boise metro alternatives.
base_profile.pcs_personality: True military town feel; the community is highly focused on supporting Gunfighter personnel.
base_profile.base_bluf: Mountain Home offers consistent value and a very low cost of living for military families.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, commute testing, childcare access, and total monthly housing cost before signing near Mountain Home.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, rental flexibility, and access to daily services around Mountain Home.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: Lead with BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in payment math for Mountain Home clients — not generic Mountain Home / Elmore County stats alone.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Mountain Home is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, and practical commute corridors.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Identify the most realistic primary gate.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window you would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Mountain Home housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH is a decision filter, not a promise of affordability.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter for Mountain Home, not a promise of affordability. Push users into PCSUnited financial calculators when the housing gap looks tight.

parser_notes: This Mountain Home AFB file was upgraded to the Lackland-style PCSUnited schema with slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
Mountain Home / Elmore County is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context; base-specific market data was retained.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.
